sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this procedure defines how {company_legal_name} receives, evaluates, investigates, and closes complaints per ISO 13485:2016 §8.2.2, 21 CFR 820.198, and EU MDR Article 87–92. Complaints feed CAPA ({ref:CA-001}), vigilance ({ref:CA-005}), and PMS ({ref:RA-005}).

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.content: Applies to all complaints received by {company_legal_name} from any source. Intake channels: {complaint_intake_channel}.

sections.title: 3. Process Steps
clauses: 3.1 Intake and logging within 1 working day on {ref:CA-004}
3.2 Initial assessment: is it a complaint? does it require regulatory reporting?
3.4 Investigation including device return/analysis where applicable
3.5 Regulatory reporting decision: MDR (FDA), Vigilance (EU MDR Annex), MHRA, etc. via {ref:CA-005}
3.6 Field Safety Corrective Action evaluation via {ref:CA-006}
sections.section_prompt: For each step, give responsible role, timeline, and required record. Include explicit timelines for serious-incident reporting (e.g. 2 days serious public-health threat, 10 days serious incident, 15 days other for EU; 30 days for MDR for FDA).

sections.title: 4. Reportability Decision Matrix
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 6–8 scenarios: death; serious deterioration; serious public-health threat; near-incident without harm; component failure without harm; cosmetic complaint; cybersecurity incident; usability error causing harm. Specify EU MDR action, FDA MDR action and statutory deadline. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 5. Metrics & Trending
sections.section_prompt: Describe trending performed on complaint data (rate per 1000 units, top failure modes, time-to-close) and how trends trigger CAPA per {ref:CA-001} and feed management review {ref:QP-005}.
